name: Pin Payments API
what_it_does: Australian payment gateway with REST API for credit card processing — providing charge API, customer vault, subscriptions, and refunds for Australian businesses with test/live environment separation and developer-friendly documentation.
use_cases: Processing AUD credit card payments via REST API for Australian ecommerce
Storing customer card details securely in card vault for recurring charges
Managing subscription billing with automatic renewal charges
Processing refunds and partial refunds programmatically via API
Building Australian payment flows for SaaS with Australian card processing
not_for: High-volume enterprise payments needing gateway redundancy
best_when: An Australian developer or startup needs a simple, clean REST API for credit card processing with good documentation and developer-friendly experience.
avoid_when: You need international currency support, non-Australian payment methods, or enterprise-scale processing.

auth.notes: HTTP Basic auth with API key as username, empty password. Separate test and live API keys. Account-scoped access. No OAuth2 or fine-grained scopes. Simple key pair model.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free test environment; production: 1.75% + $0.30 per transaction (Australian cards)
pricing.notes: No monthly fee — pay per transaction. Australian company. Competitive rates for local cards. Test environment fully functional at no cost. Good for Australian startups needing a simple payment API without Stripe's complexity.

performance.rate_limits: Standard rate limits; contact for high-volume

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Clean REST API with charges, refunds, customers, cards, and subscriptions. Basic auth with API key. Webhook for payment events. Good developer documentation. Smaller ecosystem than Stripe but well-documented for Australian use cases.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Charge creation creates new charges — no idempotency key. Refund creation creates new refunds. Customer management idempotent by email. Card storage idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: HTTP Basic auth with empty password is unusual — some HTTP clients need special handling
Australian-focused — non-AUD transactions have higher fees
No idempotency key support — implement deduplication logic for retry safety
3D Secure implementation differs from Stripe — check integration requirements

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. PCI-DSS compliant. Australian data residency. Basic auth with API key. Test/live key separation. No raw card storage — tokenized. Australian regulatory compliance. No formal SOC2 certification published.
